strategy: maximize coverage of static lnet admission validation, cross-lnet overlap behavior, immutability, reconcile status, and lifecycle edges

contextChunks.content: LNET is ARM-defined source of truth for VM networking and binds NICs to vmSwitchName present on every node.
LNET carries subnet intent including addressPrefix, ipPools, routes, VLAN, and DNS.
Static subnet mode allocates VM IPs from configured ipPools.
Dynamic subnet mode uses DHCP and can have zero subnets in current behavior.
VLAN non-zero means tagged traffic; VLAN zero or omitted means untagged.
Webhook currently allows at most one subnet; creating more than one subnet is rejected.
Valid configuration requires same vmSwitchName to exist on every node.
IP pools must lie within subnet usable host range, excluding network and broadcast addresses.
CIDR must parse as IPv4 and /31 and /32 are rejected; network and broadcast must be computable.
Gateway, if provided, must be inside subnet CIDR.
Each DNS server entry must be a valid IPv4 address.
VLAN must be integer in 0..4094; VLAN 0 or omitted is untagged.
Each pool start/end must be inside usable host range for the subnet.
Pools in same subnet can be non-contiguous but must be disjoint and non-overlapping.
Pool ranges with start greater than end are invalid.
NIC IP equal to LNET DNS or gateway is allowed only for static NIC allocation in Workload LNET and only when not infra-reserved.
Dynamic NIC IP allocation must not allow NIC IP to equal LNET DNS or gateway.
On LNET create/update, reject if configured DNS or gateway belongs to Infrastructure LNET IP pool range.
Validation must check existing NIC IPs for collision with configured DNS or gateway values.
NICs with customer-provided explicit IP should be annotated at creation to distinguish static allocation for policy decisions.
DNS server updates do not require IPPool reserved list mutation because reserved state is already managed through allocation semantics.
Overlapping IPv4 prefixes on same VLAN across LNETs must be rejected.
Overlapping IPv4 prefixes can be allowed across different VLANs.
Disjoint IPv4 prefixes on the same VLAN are allowed.
Subnet name cannot be changed after creation.
Transition from one subnet to zero subnets is rejected; subnet deletion is not allowed.
Transition from zero to one subnet with addressPrefix, ippools, ipAllocationMethod, routes, or vlan is rejected.
If LNET has aksarc-prefixed finalizer, DNS server mutation is blocked with validation error.
With AKS finalizer, no-op DNS update with identical value may be accepted.
After AKS finalizer removal, previously blocked DNS change can succeed if otherwise valid.
Reconcile is triggered by LNET add/update/delete, NIC CR changes, and NodeInventory changes.
Controller computes allNodesHaveVSwitch by checking vmSwitchName presence per node via Node Inventory.
Used and available per pool are derived from NIC CR assigned IPs intersecting each pool range.
Ready is true only when all nodes have the vSwitch and no pool is exhausted.
Status patch updates pool info.used and info.available values for each subnet pool.
IPPool ownerReferences should point to LNET so delete cascades when appropriate.
IPPool logicalNetworkArmID, cidr, and ranges should align with LNET subnet configuration.
IPPool should transition to Ready with non-zero capacity for non-empty static IPv4 pools.
IPAllocation should update pool used/available counts and advance lastAllocatedIP after allocations.
Controller logs should emphasize condition flips and reconcile outcome summaries with node counts and duration.
